sourceMovie.title: A Nightmare on Elm Street Part 2: Freddy's Revenge
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit A Nightmare on Elm Street Part 2: Freddy's Revenge for the same reason it broke through in 1985: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a horror rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Jack Sholder keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The film leans hard into its core genre, which is exactly what fans say they wanted more of from blockbusters in that era.

recommendations.title: It Follows
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If A Nightmare on Elm Street Part 2: Freddy's Revenge clicked for you, It Follows (2015) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after A Nightmare on Elm Street Part 2: Freddy's Revenge?
faq.answer: Try It Follows, Insidious: Chapter 2, Nosferatu—each shares DNA with A Nightmare on Elm Street Part 2: Freddy's Revenge while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is A Nightmare on Elm Street Part 2: Freddy's Revenge?
faq.answer: A Nightmare on Elm Street Part 2: Freddy's Revenge (1985) is best described as Horror. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is A Nightmare on Elm Street Part 2: Freddy's Revenge part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "A Nightmare on Elm Street Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
